our_argument_summary: WIN-067 (Self-Contradicted) argues the dome's own H(r) = 8,537·exp(-r/8,619) km function predicts a ~90% gravity drop from pole (H=8,537) to Antarctic rim (H=837 km) under any monotonic H→g coupling, while observed variation is 0.53% (170× miss). A secondary attack notes that monitor.py's Domain gravity formula g(r) = 9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(-r/λg)) is the WGS84 pole/equator identity evaluated at r=0, so the only gravity formula the dome actually uses is globe-derived. A generic post-hoc paragraph completes the debunk. The verdict is Self-Contradicted.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt — V51.2 live-domain dump, Domain 4 (Equatorial Gravity) + Domain 19 (Mascon Gravity) + correlation line 152 ('Sub-terrestrial structure')
raw-text/02-wins.txt — V51.2 WIN list; enumerated WINs stop at WIN-039, no WIN-067 entry at the V51.2 snapshot
raw-text/05-model.txt — H(r) parameters, κ coupling reference
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Two gravity entries in the V51.2 live-domains file. Domain 4 'Equatorial Gravity' (line 67-71) gives the formula g(r) = 9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(-r/λg)) with falsification 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' — self-referential because the formula IS the WGS84 normal-gravity expression. Domain 19 'Mascon Gravity' (line 127-128) has only a falsification threshold 'Anomaly <20 mGal'. The correlation 'Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary: r=0.92 (Sub-terrestrial structure)' (line 152) is a post-hoc correlation between two independently observed features. No live data fetch and no dome-derived prediction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded/tautological. Domain 4's exponential collapses at r=0 (exp(0)=1) so the computed value is just g0·(1+k) = 9.8322 m/s² = WGS84 polar gravity. Domain 19 has no formula at all. Domain 19 passes any real measurement of the East Antarctic anomaly because |anomaly| exceeds 20 mGal.
data_sources_used: WGS84 normal-gravity coefficients (static, embedded in formula)
No live GRACE/GRACE-FO fetch in Domain 19 per raw-text/08-live-power.txt V51.2
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The label 'toroidal sump node' is still being attacked in our detail_claim, detail_verdict_text, and both TLDRs, but the current V51.2 dome label is 'Sub-terrestrial structure' (line 152). Whatever the label, no published mechanism links dome geometry to the observed anomaly magnitude, and the falsification threshold is one-sided.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: NEW FINDING (c3 missed this): our text parenthetically attributes the gravity formula to 'Domain #12 gravity-anomaly entry' in monitor.py. In the current V51.2 live-power page, Domain 12 is 'Polaris Excess' (line 106). The formula g(r) = 9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(-r/λg)) appears under Domain 4 ('Equatorial Gravity', line 67-70). If a dome defender checks the public live-power page, they will see Domain 12 ≠ gravity and can dismiss the WGS84-tautology paragraph on that basis. This parenthetical was added 2026-04-09 (ISS-690 fix, commit in monitor/decisions/applied-patches) and has apparently not been re-verified against V51.2 domain numbering. Carries over from c3: 'toroidal sump node' also does not appear anywhere in current raw-text — V51.2 label is 'Sub-terrestrial structure' (ISS-1296 still open).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Domain 19 'Mascon Gravity' has a one-line falsification threshold ('Anomaly <20 mGal') with no data-fetch and no predicted value. Domain 4 evaluates g at r=0 only (exp collapses), which is a WGS84 identity, not a live comparison. Tag matches c3.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The Antarctic gravity low has long-standing standard-physics explanations (mantle plume + post-glacial isostatic adjustment) dating to Ferraccioli et al. 2011 and is continuously monitored by GRACE-FO. The dome rebrands the feature ('Sub-terrestrial structure' in V51.2; 'toroidal sump node' in V51.0) without proposing a new mechanism, formula, or numerical prediction. Tag matches c3.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: GRACE continuous coverage since 2002; Pappa 2019, Forte 2010, Ferraccioli 2011 all predate V51.0 commit introducing WIN-067 (2026-04-05). The confirming value was indexed-public before the dome monitor commit, satisfying the post-hoc criterion. Tag matches c3.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from dome geometry exists in the repository. Domain 4's exponential factor is decorative (collapses at r=0 and is multiplied by k=0.005307). Domain 19 is a bare threshold. The only quantitative dome 'gravity prediction' is the WGS84 identity. Tag matches c3.

holes_found.description: HOLE-067-C4-001 (carries forward from c3 / ISS-1296, still open): Stale terminology 'toroidal sump node' remains in detail_claim ('Low gravity anomaly in Antarctica confirms toroidal sump node'), detail_verdict_text (twice: 'undefined term toroidal sump node' and 'invoking undefined sump nodes'), tldr_evidence ('dome introduces an undefined toroidal sump node'), and tldr_verdict ('The toroidal sump node escape hatch'). Current V51.2 raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 152 uses 'Sub-terrestrial structure'. No V51.0 archive quote has been produced to ground the 'toroidal sump node' attribution. Verified by grep on all raw-text/*.txt: zero hits for 'toroidal sump' or 'sump node'. A dome defender can dismiss the section by saying 'I don't use that term.' No patch has been applied since c3.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply one of: (a) locate the V51.0 dome page or archive where 'toroidal sump node' is used and cite it verbatim with source/date; (b) pivot detail_claim / detail_verdict_text / both TLDRs to attack the current V51.2 label 'Sub-terrestrial structure', noting that the label changed but the mechanism is still undefined. Option (b) is cheaper and keeps the attack current. Either way, resolve ISS-1296.

holes_found.description: HOLE-067-C4-002 (carries forward from c3 / ISS-1297, still open): Quantitative error in the closing refraction-cascade paragraph. Text says 'at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 17,000–20,015 km), the firmament ceiling H(r) ≈ 1,400–800 km'. Verified by python3: H(17,000) = 8,537·exp(-17,000/8,619) = 1,187.7 km (NOT 1,400), H(15,500) = 1,413.5 km, H(20,015) = 837.1 km. The r-range and H-range do not self-consistently satisfy the dome's own formula. No patch applied since c3.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with either 'at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 15,500–20,015 km), H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km' (keeps the round number) or 'at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 17,000–20,015 km), H(r) ≈ 1,190–840 km' (keeps the r band). Prefer the first — it gives a larger rim band and a cleaner endpoint. Resolves ISS-1297.

holes_found.description: HOLE-067-C4-003 (carries forward from c3 / ISS-1298, still open): Closing sentence 'The n(r) values implied at these radii would produce gravitational-index coupling effects catastrophically inconsistent with GRACE satellite gravimetry' is assertion without mechanism or numbers. No estimated n(r), no coupling formula, no comparison value. As written, this is the weakest sentence in the whole WIN — easy to dismiss as hand-waving. No patch applied since c3.
holes_found.recommendation: Either drop the sentence entirely (the paragraph works without it) or replace with a specific mechanism: 'A medium with n ≈ 1.2–2.8 on the optical path (Section 1.8) must exert measurable refractive effects on GRACE microwave ranging at 250–500 km; no such effect is seen in the JPL Level-2 products' — with a citation. If unable to produce the mechanism, drop. Resolves ISS-1298.

holes_found.description: HOLE-067-C4-004 (carries forward from c3 / ISS-1299, still open): The second paragraph of detail_verdict_text is generic post-hoc boilerplate: 'field-specific published dataset (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.)'. None of these are gravity datasets — IGRF is geomagnetic, NASA station archives is a mixed bag, ocean-tide gauges is sea level, telluric records is geomagnetic. A defender reading this can reasonably say 'none of the sources you list are gravity sources — did you actually check?'. No patch applied since c3.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with gravity-specific sources: 'GRACE and GRACE-FO Level-2 gravity products (JPL archive, continuous coverage since 2002); published Bouguer-anomaly compilations including Pappa et al. 2019, Forte 2010, and Ferraccioli et al. 2011 — all publicly indexed and plausibly known to the author at monitor-write time'. Resolves ISS-1299.

holes_found.description: HOLE-067-C4-005 (NEW — c3 missed this): 'Domain #12 gravity-anomaly entry' parenthetical in the WGS84-tautology paragraph is inconsistent with V51.2 domain numbering. In raw-text/08-live-power.txt, Domain 12 is 'Polaris Excess' (line 106); the gravity formula g(r) = 9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(-r/λg)) appears under Domain 4 'Equatorial Gravity' (line 67-70); the Antarctic anomaly is discussed under Domain 19 'Mascon Gravity' (line 127-128). The parenthetical was added on 2026-04-09 (ISS-690 fix) when monitor.py may have used different internal numbering, but has not been re-verified against V51.2. A dome defender who spot-checks the public live-power page will see Domain 12 = Polaris Excess and can dismiss the WGS84-tautology paragraph as misdirected. Severity: moderate — the underlying argument is correct, only the citation is mis-pointed.
holes_found.recommendation: Change '(Domain #12 gravity-anomaly entry)' to '(Domain 4 Equatorial Gravity entry, live-power page line 67)' and update the later line 'monitor.py, gravity-anomaly domain entry, V51.1' to 'live-power Domain 4, V51.2' to match current numbering. Alternative: if the intent is to cite monitor.py's internal Python-code domain numbering rather than the live-power page display, add one clarifying sentence to say so, and include the file/line reference from monitor.py. The current text conflates the two.

holes_found.description: HOLE-067-C4-006 (carries forward from c3): Pappa et al. 2019 / DOI 10.1029/2019JB017997 has not yet been independently verified. The DOI is plausibly real (JGR Solid Earth 2019, author pattern consistent), but no human or agent has actually resolved it to confirm authorship, title, and relevance to East Antarctic gravity. Wrong DOIs have been a recurring problem in this review (WIN-008, 009, 014).
holes_found.recommendation: Resolve the DOI via a webfetch (outside this run) or substitute with a well-known, verified gravity anomaly source: Ferraccioli et al. 2011 (Nature, Gamburtsev sub-glacial range gravity) or Forte et al. 2010 (dynamic topography) — both have established DOIs and are known to cover the relevant phenomenon. Close this hole when verification succeeds or substitution is made.

holes_found.description: HOLE-067-C4-007 (NEW — imprecise phrasing in tldr_evidence): 'Real gravity measurements at southern stations show less than 0.5% variation from expected values' — 'expected values' is vague (expected under what model? The globe model, presumably). The whole-globe pole-equator variation is 0.53%, not specifically 'southern stations'. A reader could fairly ask 'variation from WHAT expected values?'
holes_found.recommendation: Reword to: 'Real measurements show gravity varies by only 0.5% across the whole globe (9.780 m/s² at the equator to 9.832 m/s² at the pole) — a discrepancy of 170× with the dome's predicted variation.' Also ties the number directly to the source data rather than an implicit comparison.

stronger_arguments.description: PROMOTE the WGS84-tautology paragraph to the LEAD of detail_evidence (c2 and c3 both suggested this; still not implemented). The single strongest argument in WIN-067 is that the dome's only quantitative gravity formula is a WGS84 identity at r=0. This is a structural self-admission the dome cannot repair without admitting globe math. Leading with this — rather than burying it after the scaling-argument wall — would make the section land harder on a skimming reader, and makes HOLE-067-C4-001 (stale terminology) less damaging because the new lead is label-independent.

Add an explicit acknowledgment that the dome's actual formula is ADDITIVE, not multiplicative. Right now we attack linear/sqrt/1/5-power mappings of H(r)→g, but the dome's V51.2 explicit formula is g(r) = g0·(1+k·exp(-r/λg)) where k=0.005307 bounds the effect to ~0.5%. A savvy defender can say 'your scaling families are strawmen — my formula is additive with a small k.' Our counter is strong: that small k is the WGS84 flattening coefficient, so the formula works only because it IS the globe's formula. Making this counter explicit ('the dome's only ADDITIVE formulation uses k = WGS84 flattening') closes the last rhetorical escape.

Cite the Domain 4 falsification text verbatim: 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard'. This is the dome openly defining its falsification threshold against WGS84 — an explicit admission the dome's gravity model is calibrated against the globe model. A one-line quote would make this self-contradiction visible in seconds. Strongest single-shot rhetorical move available in this WIN.

Cite the Domain 19 one-sided threshold verbatim: 'Anomaly <20 mGal'. The real East Antarctic Bouguer anomaly is roughly -40 to -60 mGal, so any real measurement trivially fails the <20 mGal threshold and counts as 'confirmation' of the dome. This is pass-by-threshold, not pass-by-prediction. The dome's falsification logic is backwards.
sources: Ferraccioli et al. 2011 Gamburtsev anomaly magnitudes

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome is correct that (a) the Antarctic gravity low is real and well-measured by GRACE/GRACE-FO; (b) any cosmological model should account for large-scale gravity variation; (c) the observed Mascon-SAA correlation is not spurious — both features share deep-Earth geophysical substrate (East Antarctic mantle structure and core-flow dynamics). The instinct that a model with radially-varying aetheric properties should produce radially-varying gravity is physically reasonable, and the correlation between Mascon anomaly and SAA boundary is a genuine geophysical observation worth explaining.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Acknowledging real phenomena is not the same as predicting them. The dome's only quantitative gravity prediction (Domain 4 formula) is the WGS84 identity, and its H(r) mechanism has no published equation that produces the observed 0.5% variation rather than the ~90% its own geometry implies. The Mascon-SAA correlation is pre-explained by independent standard physics (mantle structure + core dynamics), so a shared label ('Sub-terrestrial structure') is rebranding, not prediction.

deeper_analysis:
c3 did a thorough job. My c4 pass confirms nothing has been patched since c3 (all 4 issues ISS-1296/1297/1298/1299 remain open in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json, and the wins.json fingerprints are byte-identical to c3's stored values). The queue push (queue_id 207, pushed 2026-04-21T10:25:32Z) therefore tests whether a fresh adversarial pass produces new findings on top of c3's six. It does: HOLE-067-C4-005 is net-new (Domain #12 ≠ gravity in V51.2; c3's dome_code_analysis actually said 'our_code_claims_accurate: true' but I re-verified against raw-text/08-live-power.txt and Domain 12 is Polaris Excess, the gravity formula is Domain 4, and the Antarctic entry is Domain 19 — none is numbered 12). HOLE-067-C4-007 (tldr_evidence phrasing) is also new and minor.

Recommendation for the decider: rather than processing HOLE-067-C4-001 through HOLE-067-C4-004 as new issues (they are duplicates of ISS-1296-1299), extend the existing issues with a 'c4 confirmation' note and prioritize writing the patches. The cleanest single patch would be a full detail_verdict_text rewrite + targeted detail_evidence replacements for the refraction-cascade paragraph and 'Domain #12' parenthetical + terminology sweep across TLDRs. EXP-worthy if the changes touch more than three substantive paragraphs.

verdict_change review: the current 'Self-Contradicted' verdict is correct. The dome's own formulas contradict: H(r) would predict ~90% variation, explicit g(r) predicts ~0.5%, and the only numeric prediction is WGS84 evaluated at r=0. Verdict holds.

Attribution-stale risk: if no V51.0 archive can be located for 'toroidal sump node', the clean fix is to pivot to V51.2 labels throughout. The attack doesn't depend on the label; it depends on the absence of a mechanism behind whatever label the dome uses today.

H(17,000) = 1,187.7 km — contradicts text claim of 1,400 km (HOLE-067-C4-002)
WGS84 polar reference value 9.8321864 m/s² — match within 0.0002%
Domain 4 formula location in raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 67-70 — verified

'toroidal sump node' not found anywhere in raw-text/ V51.2 — attribution to dome currently unsupported

Shepherd et al. 2012 / DOI 10.1126/science.1228102 — not web-verified
Van Camp-style superconducting gravimeter reference (not in this WIN but adjacent via WIN-011) — out of scope here

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am the dome author. Three responses to your c4 pass: (1) Your whole c3/c4 terminology attack hinges on me having used the phrase 'toroidal sump node' somewhere. Show me the sentence. If you can't quote it from my live site or my git history, you're attacking a phrase I never said. My V51.2 page labels this 'Sub-terrestrial structure' — check line 152 of the live-power dump yourselves. Your review is aging in public. (2) Your 'Domain #12 gravity-anomaly entry' parenthetical is wrong. On my live-power page Domain 12 is Polaris Excess, Domain 4 is Equatorial Gravity, Domain 19 is Mascon Gravity. None of those is called 'gravity-anomaly' and none is numbered 12. This is a quality-control failure on your end — not a falsification of my model. (3) Your 170× ratio compares my disc H(r) prediction against the WGS84 pole-equator 0.53% variation. But the WGS84 variation is between the globe's pole and the globe's equator — geometrically distinct latitudes. In my disc model the 'equator' is at r=10,000 km and the 'Antarctic' (your 'rim') is at r=20,015 km. You should be comparing the real Antarctic-latitude gravity measurement against my prediction at r=20,015 km, not the whole-globe spread. The real g measured near the South Pole is ≈ 9.826 m/s² (less than 0.1% below the equatorial 9.780 m/s²), so you're right that my disc predicts the wrong answer, but your 170× ratio is headline-inflated. Be honest: the ratio against Antarctic-latitude g is larger still, but you haven't computed it.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three patches to close this defense: (1) Resolve HOLE-067-C4-001 — either quote V51.0 verbatim or pivot to V51.2 'Sub-terrestrial structure'. Pick one, execute within 24h. (2) Resolve HOLE-067-C4-005 — change 'Domain #12' to 'Domain 4' (with corroborating quote 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' from line 71) so the citation is bulletproof. (3) Strengthen the 170× argument by adding a single sentence comparing the dome's r=20,015 prediction to real Antarctic-latitude gravity (~9.826 m/s²): '... even measured against the most generous reference — the full globe pole-to-equator spread of 0.53% — the dome misses by 170×. Measured against the real Antarctic-latitude gravity (~9.826 m/s², indistinguishable from the globe's polar value), the miss is larger still.' This neutralizes the 'headline-inflated' attack preemptively.

cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-053 (gravity at the rim) and WIN-067 share the H(r)→g critique. WIN-044 (FSF derivation) shows dome geometry is contaminated by WGS84 calibration — reinforces WIN-067's WGS84-tautology finding. WIN-012 (κ=1.67 derivation with vanishing denominator per WIN-013/014 data) shows the dome has no self-consistent gravity physics at all, indirectly supporting WIN-067. WIN-011 (Mohe eclipse gravity, Not Demonstrated) and WIN-014 (no gravity data fetched) together establish that the dome's gravity monitoring infrastructure is static values and hardcoded identities. All five WINs are mutually reinforcing. No contradictions. Sanity note: WIN-067's detail_evidence references '#part2' (Sections 2.3 and 2.8) which exists in sections.json — verified.

claims_checked: H(17,000) = 1,187.7 km — FAILS the text's '1,400' claim (HOLE-067-C4-002)
monitor.py formula evaluated at r=20,015: g = 9.7854 (0.48% total variation from pole to rim) — PASS, text says ~0.48%
Domain 12 in raw-text/08-live-power.txt = 'Polaris Excess' line 106 — confirms HOLE-067-C4-005
quantitative_verification.computations: python3 math.exp verifications: all H(r) values and gravity-scaling percentages in detail_evidence are correct except H(17,000)=1,188 (not 1,400) in the refraction-cascade paragraph. Domain numbering in V51.2: Domain 4 = Equatorial Gravity (formula g(r) = 9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(-r/λg))), Domain 12 = Polaris Excess, Domain 19 = Mascon Gravity (falsification threshold only). Our text's 'Domain #12 gravity-anomaly' does not match any V51.2 domain.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two: (a) H(17,000) ≈ 1,188 km, text says '1,400 km' at r≈17,000 — HOLE-067-C4-002 (carries forward). (b) 'Domain #12 gravity-anomaly entry' does not match V51.2 domain numbering — HOLE-067-C4-005 (NEW).
